name: Wayfair Mastercard®

verdict.text: The 7% back across the Wayfair family of brands (Wayfair, Joss & Main, AllModern, Birch Lane, Perigold) is genuinely strong for furniture/home spend, but the 1–3% rates elsewhere are unremarkable and rewards only redeem as Wayfair credit — worth it mainly if you furnish a home over time and don't mind store-locked rewards.

earn_rates.category: Wayfair, Joss & Main, AllModern, Birch Lane & Perigold (5% instead if using promotional financing on that purchase)

earn_note: This is the open-loop Mastercard version of Wayfair's card — it earns outside the Wayfair family of brands, unlike the closed-loop, store-only Wayfair Credit Card, which earns the same 7%/5% at Wayfair brands but nothing elsewhere. Reward Dollars are capped at a maximum $2,500 redeemable per month across participating Wayfair sites. New cardholders get $40 off a first qualifying order of $250+. Citi has issued this program since 2020, after it moved from Comenity Bank.

redemption_options.method: Applied automatically in cart/checkout at Wayfair, Joss & Main, AllModern, Birch Lane, or Perigold

points.per_100k: Not applicable — Wayfair Rewards accrue and redeem in dollars directly (no separate points conversion), capped at $2,500 redeemable per month
points.note: Reward Dollars have no cash value and can't be redeemed anywhere outside the Wayfair family of brands. Rewards never expire while the account stays open, but the $2,500/month redemption cap matters for anyone making a large furniture purchase and trying to use accumulated rewards toward it in one go.

transfer_partners.highlight: No transfer partners — Wayfair Reward Dollars redeem only across the Wayfair family of brands.

insurance.detail: Not confirmed on Citi's or Wayfair's public product pages for this card
Not confirmed on Citi's or Wayfair's public product pages for this card

protection_note: A standard (not World Elite) Mastercard tier with no confirmed purchase protection, extended warranty, or travel insurance. Treat this as a rewards-and-financing card with no meaningful insurance suite — a real gap for a card marketed around furniture and home-goods purchases that could otherwise benefit from purchase protection or extended warranty.
rental_note: No rental car coverage found for this card.

services.name: Special financing offers
services.detail: Promotional financing plans available across participating Wayfair sites on qualifying items, selectable at checkout — using promotional financing on a purchase drops the earn rate from 7% to 5% on that purchase.

services.name: Foreign transaction fee
services.detail: 3% of the U.S. dollar amount of each purchase made outside the US, per Wayfair's cardholder terms.

additional_cards.title: Authorized users

benefits.text: Earns rewards on their spend (into your account)
No dedicated insurance to share — this card doesn't carry a confirmed protection suite

additional_cards.note: Free to add, useful mainly for pooling a household's home-goods spend toward the 7% category.

timeline.text: Wayfair's credit card program moved from Comenity Bank to Citibank, N.A., which currently issues both the closed-loop Wayfair Credit Card and this open-loop Wayfair Mastercard.

Wayfair/Citi issue two distinct card products: the closed-loop, store-only Wayfair Credit Card (7%/5% at Wayfair brands, unusable elsewhere) and this open-loop Wayfair Mastercard (same 7%/5% at Wayfair brands, plus 3%/2%/1% elsewhere). Confirm which one you're comparing — they are not the same card.
